note: Publicly marketed powered-land listings and market signals compiled from broker pages, press releases, and news coverage. Coordinates are approximate (city/county-level) unless precision says 'exact'. Prices shown only where publicly stated.

sites.name: Childress Powered Land (Hwy 287)
sites.power_notes: Multiple 345kV and 138kV lines traverse the area; grid power via the Tesla substation. Flat site, fully outside floodplain.
sites.broker: CBRE (Brant Bernet, Chris Herrmann, Phoebe Bernet)

sites.name: South Dallas DC Development (Waxahachie)
sites.power_notes: 138kV and 345kV infrastructure upgrades approved by ERCOT (Oncor); pathway to hundreds of MW. Water agreement + TPDES sewer permit in place.
sites.broker: CBRE (Brant Bernet, Chris Herrmann, Phoebe Bernet)

sites.name: West Texas Panhandle 5,000-Acre AI Campus
sites.power_notes: Three power lanes: 250MW behind-the-meter ramp from mid-2026, 250MW wind offtake from Jul 2026, high-pressure gas line supporting 500MW on-site generation in 12 months (ramp to 1GW). Own 345kV frontage; can loop into SPP.
sites.fiber_notes: Zayo and AT&T fiber backbone; municipal water access

sites.name: North Abilene Tract A (1,450± ac)
sites.power_notes: Between two major data centers under construction near Abilene (Stargate corridor).

sites.name: North Abilene Tract B (1,630± ac, on-site substation)
sites.power_notes: Existing electric substation and transmission lines in NW portion of property; between two data centers under construction.

sites.name: Pineywoods (855 ac, East Texas)
sites.power_notes: ~640 buildable acres served by three HV transmission lines (345kV, 138kV, 69kV) and five major interstate natural gas pipelines; supports front-of-meter and behind-the-meter.

sites.name: DFW 1GW Ground-Lease (4×250MW blocks)
sites.power_notes: 1GW ground-lease opportunity subdivided into four 250MW blocks scheduled to energize 2028.

sites.name: DFW Strategic 1,500-Acre Hyperscale Parcel
sites.power_notes: Positioned for hyperscale campus development; 345kV transmission frontage tracts in broker portfolio.

sites.name: 88-Acre Substation-Adjacent Site (Waco–Austin corridor)
sites.power_notes: Substation adjacency; outside city jurisdiction (ETJ advantages). Marketed for crypto/AI training workloads.

sites.name: West Texas 82-Acre Edge/Modular AI Site
sites.power_notes: Marketed for edge or modular AI deployment.

sites.name: Aquila Site (Hill County, 800+ ac)
sites.power_notes: Four contiguous parcels under contract at ~$100k/acre (~14× appraisal) for proposed data center development.

sites.name: Amazon Data Services — Bastrop County (1,300+ ac acquired)
sites.power_notes: One of the largest Central Texas land assemblies tied to hyperscale development (May 2026).

sites.name: Tract — Caldwell County Technology Park (1,515 ac, multi-GW)
sites.power_notes: Master-planned multi-gigawatt data center technology park between Austin and San Antonio.

sites.name: CloudBurst — San Marcos (706 ac, 1.2GW approved)
sites.power_notes: 1.2GW campus approvals; ~$14.5B projected investment.

sites.name: APR Energy — Pampa (800 ac contracted + 500 ac ROFR)
sites.power_notes: On-site gas turbines planned; phase 1 targeted early 2026.

sites.name: Advanced Energy & Intelligence Campus — Amarillo (5,800 ac)
sites.power_notes: 18M sq ft of data centers planned; gas, solar, wind and nuclear power mix.

sites.name: Black Mountain Power — Bowie County (600 ac acquired)
sites.power_notes: Fort Worth company purchased ~600 ac (Mar 2026) for data center development.

sites.name: Stargate (Crusoe/OpenAI) — Abilene anchor campus
sites.power_notes: Anchor hyperscale AI campus driving the Abilene powered-land corridor.

sites.name: Galaxy Helios — West Texas (1.6GW+, 1,500+ ac)
sites.power_notes: Former bitcoin mine converted to AI data center campus; grown to 1.6GW+ in 2026 — the template for West Texas powered-land conversions.

sites.name: Stream Data Centers — Graham Project (ex-Plug Power, 164MW interconnect)
sites.power_notes: Plug Power sold its Graham, TX land and associated 164MW of grid interconnection assets to Stream Data Centers for up to $76.5M ($50M at closing, up to $26.5M tied to confirmed load capacity in the final Texas utility interconnection agreement).
sites.status: Sale agreement signed; closing expected on/about Jul 31, 2026

sites.name: Big Digital Energy / 10NetZero JV — Hood County Site
sites.power_notes: Powered industrial site with 17MW operational power today, expandable to 111MW grid power pending ERCOT validation. On-site natural gas pipelines (two 12-inch, one 20-inch) allow behind-the-meter generation for a buildout to up to 311MW total. 30,000+ sq ft of existing structures to be repurposed for AI data center use.
sites.status: 50/50 JV + signed letter of intent to acquire (Jul 6, 2026)

sites.name: MARA — Matagorda County Site (ex-HIF USA, up to 2GW)
sites.power_notes: Definitive agreement with HIF USA for a large-scale powered land site ~90 miles SW of Houston; access to up to 1GW of grid capacity by Oct 2027, up to 2GW by Apr 2028. To be developed with Starwood Digital Ventures for HPC/flexible compute including Bitcoin mining.

sites.name: AmpZ Champion / EPG Champion — Angelina County (former Southland Paper Mill)
sites.power_notes: Memorandum of option for ~1,041 acres across multiple abstracts/surveys at the former Southland Paper Mill site; nearby land could expand the project to ~4,000 acres. Part of the site to be used as a sound/wetland buffer. Estimated $1.05B private capital investment if finalized.
sites.status: Option agreement / under development and community negotiation (Jul 2026)
